sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Clicking the bottom green square caused a change (board_changed: True, 20 cells changed in bbox [56, 17, 61, 22]). Looking at the image, the green square now has a white border around it, indicating it's been "selected". This is a selection mechanism. Now I need to figure out what to do next - likely click on a target location to place the selected color. Let me check the current state and try clicking on one of the gray squares in the middle area.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The green square got selected (white border). Now let me check the current state
# and try clicking on a gray square in the middle to place the color.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Check if the green square now has a white border
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] == 16:
        print(f"Green square: id={n['id']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check what changed
print("\nChecking for new white border around selected square...")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 5:
        print(f"  White object: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Let me now try clicking on the first gray square in the middle to place the green color
# Gray square 14 is at [[29, 22], [29, 23], [30, 23], [30, 22]]
print("\nTrying to click on first gray square in middle area to place green...")
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 22}])
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
